comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS-1 review note (G6 — approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection): No G6 findings. This chunk is the docs-only ticket "Docs: gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern". G6 is effectively not-applicable: documentation authoring is a well-understood activity with no structurally-different "better approach" to coach (no data-layer / control-flow / dependency-graph alternative exists for "write docs about X"). The plan also affirmatively satisfies the criterion's correctness concern: its own AC#4 ("States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check)") directly forecloses the misleading-latency anti-pattern that 5fd2's round-5 review flagged (presenting the ~50ms fast-claim number as user-facing time-to-first-work when the heavy review runs out-of-band, ~minutes for a heavy epic). The approach correctly anchors on the canonical workflow-engine doc in 6f2d (WS-DOC) by cross-reference rather than duplicating content, avoiding a divergence/duplication anti-pattern, and states a positive rationale ("the gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented"). Clean for G6.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — rubric T10 (Infrastructure / IaC, overlay):

NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. This is a documentation-only ticket. The Scope explicitly states "Docs only; the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d," and the What/AC sections cover only documenting gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, the CI-check pattern, and the Pass-4 coach. The plan provisions/configures no cloud resources, IaC (Terraform/CFN/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible), or Kubernetes/Helm, so the T10 overlay does not apply.

Affirmations (T10): not-applicable for this non-infra docs ticket — no findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (G6 — approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) for the docs-only plan "Docs: gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern" (cross-referenced here as the Pass-4 coach doc target).

VERDICT: No G6 findings — clean.

Reasoning (grounded):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: The mechanism is "author docs covering behavior / config (incl. the disable kill-switch) / fail-open / cert semantics / CI pattern, cross-referencing 6f2d WS-DOC." For a docs deliverable the load-bearing correctness risk is honesty/completeness, and AC#4 ("States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check)") explicitly forecloses the misleading-latency anti-pattern flagged in 5fd2's review (presenting the ~50ms fast-claim number as user-facing time-to-first-work while heavy review runs out-of-band). Sound.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the named problem ("the gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented") rather than a proxy — the ACs map 1:1 to the topics requiring honest documentation, including the Pass-4 affirmative coach (move registry + how coaching is delivered + advisory-only), consistent with 75a9's advisory/single-turn/reference-by-id design.
- APPROACH SELECTION: the only structurally-different choice for docs-only work is duplicate-the-workflow-engine-content vs cross-reference. The plan chose cross-reference ("the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d"), the correct call — it avoids a divergence/duplication anti-pattern and respects 6f2d WS-DOC as single source of truth. Defensible; generated alternatives discarded (not written into the plan).
- POSITIVE RATIONALE: present ("must be honestly documented" for a broad client base).

ANTI-FP: documentation authoring is a well-understood activity with no genuine structurally-different "better approach" to coach beyond the duplicate-vs-reference question the plan already handles. G6's alternative-generation requirement is effectively not-applicable. Clean for G6.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — rubric T3 (Integration feasibility, overlay):

NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. The T3 overlay applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a capability it has not used before. This is a documentation-only ticket — Scope: "Docs only; the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d." The What/AC sections only describe authoring docs (gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, the CI-check pattern, and the Pass-4 affirmative coach) and cross-referencing existing internal artifacts (6f2d WS-DOC, 75a9). No external CLI/API/service/library is being integrated and no never-before-used capability is asserted; the cross-references are to internal rebar tickets (already-used / internal), which the overlay calls not-applicable. There are no named external subcommands/endpoints to verify, and no auth/HTTPS preconditions to state because no service is contacted.

Affirmations (T3): not-applicable for this docs-only chunk — no findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, overlay):

NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings. T1 is an overlay that fires only when the plan crosses a bright-line (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, a novel architectural pattern, a performance/scalability target, or a migration). This is a DOCS-ONLY ticket ("Scope: Docs only; the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d"); its deliverable is documentation describing already-decided behavior (three-pass gate, config surface incl. the kill-switch, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern, and the Pass-4 coach). Writing docs about an existing system is a well-trodden activity with no novel pattern to justify, so the overlay's ANTI-FP clause ("not-applicable when no bright-line fires") applies.

(a) Prior art / reinvention: the load-bearing architectural decisions are NOT made in this ticket — they live in sibling/parent tickets where prior art was already weighed: the engine build/agent-runtime/visual-editing decisions (6f2d → e6d9, with a build-vs-buy trail across DBOS/Restate/SpiffWorkflow/BPMN/Pydantic-AI), and the Pass-4 coach design itself (75a9: registry pattern reused from the criteria/detector registries, LLM-feedback research grounding — Huang/DeepMind external-grounded > self-reflection, anti-sycophancy, lost-in-the-middle). This ticket DOCUMENTS those decisions and cross-references 6f2d (WS-DOC), so it neither reinvents nor repackages — it points at the canonical anchor rather than duplicating it. (b) No novel pattern is introduced here to justify. (c) No unverified capability assertions ("library supports X") are made by this docs ticket; such assertions belong to the build tickets.

Affirmations (T1): correctly bounds itself to docs and defers all architecture to 6f2d/e6d9/75a9 via cross-reference (anti-duplication); no bright-line crossing introduced — overlay not-applicable.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS 1 (T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety, overlay): NOT-APPLICABLE. The REVIEW_RESULT sidecar is an additive, append-only, reducer-ignored event with no backfill of existing data and no blocking DDL; rollout follows rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones / readers-before-writers pattern. Clean chunk — no findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: FAIL**

Two requirements are not met: the move registry's project-extensibility is explicitly deferred, and the AC6 test coverage for the `pass4_coach` function is incomplete (finding→move mapping on a labelled set, deterministic render, reference resolution, and the no-match-yields-no-coaching case are not tested).

---

### AC1 — Single-turn structured-output call; `{move_id, subject, finding_refs}` clustered by move; LLM authors no free prose
**MET.** `pass4_coach()` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:493` runs a single `execution_mode="single_turn"` structured call using the `_pass4_model` schema (`CoachNote` with `move_id`, `subject`, `finding_refs`). Prose is rendered deterministically from the locked template at line 533; the LLM never authors free prose.

### AC2 — Move registry (declarative, project-extensible) with moves 1-9,11,12 and LOCKED imperative templates; deterministic rendering
**NOT MET (partially).** `MOVE_REGISTRY` at `orchestrator.py:55-100` contains all 11 required move entries (1-9, 11, 12) with locked imperative templates, and rendering is deterministic (`move["template"].format(subject=subject)`). However, the project-extensibility requirement is **explicitly deferred**: the comment at `orchestrator.py:52` reads `"project-extensible via .rebar later — child 75a9"` — meaning the `.rebar`-based extension mechanism the ticket and AC specify has not been implemented.

### AC3 — C1-C4 guardrails enforced by construction, including SUBJECT VALIDATOR
**MET.** `_validate_subject()` at `passes.py:559-570` enforces the bounded noun-phrase constraint (≤8 words / ≤60 chars), rejects code tokens (backticks, parentheses, `;`/`=`), and rejects leading imperative verbs. Tests at `test_plan_review.py:265-272` confirm acceptance and rejection behavior.

### AC4 — Coaching `finding_refs` reference the stable per-finding `id` minted by orchestrator (`ca03`); findings + coaching ship in one result
**MET.** `mint_finding_id()` at `orchestrator.py:154` is the sole owner of the content-fingerprint id. Finding ids are stamped at assembly (lines 480-513), and the coaching list (line 566) and findings (lines 564-565) ship together in the same result dict. The sidecar at `sidecar.py:126-129` also records coaching with `finding_refs`.

### AC5 — Advisory only; coaching never blocks and never alters Pass-3 decision; shared with `9da1`
**MET.** The coach runs after Pass-3 at `orchestrator.py:526-538`, exceptions are absorbed into `coverage["coach_error"]`, and the result dict never uses coaching to set `verdict`, `blocking`, or `advisory`. The `passes.py` module doc (line 4) notes this is the shared coaching layer for `9da1`.

### AC6 — Tested: finding→move mapping on labelled set; deterministic render; reference resolution; no-match yields no coaching
**NOT MET.** Only the subject validator is unit-tested (`test_subject_validator_accepts_noun_phrase`, `test_subject_validator_rejects_imperative_code_and_overlong`). The `pass4_coach()` function itself has **no unit tests** covering:
- finding→move mapping on a labelled set (no test calls `pass4_coach()` with a `FakeRunner` and asserts move selection)
- deterministic render (no test verifies that `template.format(subject=subject)` produces the expected coaching string)
- reference resolution (no test verifies that `finding_refs` in the coaching output point to ids that exist in the advisory list)
- no-match behavior (no test asserts that a finding to which no move applies produces no coaching entry)

The eval-specs directory (`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`) contains specs for `plan-review-finder`, `plan-review-verifier`, and `plan-review-isf-finder`, but **not** `plan-review-coach`. `tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py` does not include `"plan-review-coach"` in `PLAN_REVIEW_SPECS`.

---

**Summary:** The core mechanism (single-turn structured call, locked move templates, subject validator, finding id contract, advisory-only) is implemented. Two requirements remain unmet: (1) the project-extensible `.rebar` override path for the move registry is explicitly deferred per the comment at `orchestrator.py:52`, and (2) the AC6 test coverage for `pass4_coach` is incomplete — the labelled-set mapping, deterministic render, reference resolution, and no-match-yields-no-coaching cases are not tested at any level.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture justification, overlay): **PASS, no findings.**

T1 is an overlay that fires only on a bright-line (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel architectural pattern, perf/scalability target, or migration). None fires here, and the closest candidate (novel pattern) is decisively resolved by the plan's own text + the codebase:

AFFIRMATIONS:
- T1(a) prior art considered / not reinventing: The plan explicitly reuses established in-repo patterns rather than inventing — "the same cheap shape as the Pass-2 aggregate verifier" (single-turn structured-output) and MOVE REGISTRY "same pattern as the criteria/detector registries." Verified in code: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:56` (MOVE_REGISTRY), the shared `rebar.llm.runner` seam, and the Pass-1/2/3 framework documented in `docs/plan-review-gate.md:73-81`. The move catalog is a set of well-established named SE plays (spike, pre-mortem, ADR/one-way-door, riskiest-assumption-test, etc.).
- T1(b) novelty justified vs established approach: The Scope section grounds the design in named external prior art — "external-grounded feedback > self-reflection (Huang/DeepMind); anti-sycophancy; lost-in-the-middle; specific-actionable > Socratic" — and explicitly states the rejected alternative ("NOT human-pedagogy (no productive-struggle)"). This is anti-repackaging done well; no unjustified novel pattern.
- T1(c) unverified capability assertions resolved: The load-bearing capability claim — reuse of the rebar.llm single-turn structured-output path — is real (confirmed at the runner seam; Pass 2 already uses it). No "library supports X" assertion is left unverified.

Per T1 ANTI-FP ("a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search; not-applicable when no bright-line fires"), this chunk is clean for T1.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: PASS / not-applicable. No findings.

Rationale (grounded):
- The T3 overlay applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a previously-unused capability. This plan does neither.
- The Pass-4 coach explicitly REUSES an internal, already-used integration: "Reuses the rebar.llm single-turn structured-output path" / "the same cheap shape as the Pass-2 aggregate verifier." Verified in the codebase:
  - `src/rebar/llm/structured.py` — the single-turn structured-output reliability stack (output_mode, tolerant_parse, validate_to, bounded retry) is present.
  - `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` — the Runner seam exposes the generalized `structured` output `mode` + `output_schema` already (RunRequest), with a FakeRunner for offline exercise.
  - `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` — Pass-2 is described as the existing single-turn aggregate verifier through the same Runner seam; Pass 4 is documented as the same shape.
  - `src/rebar/llm/aggregate.py` — the deterministic post-LLM clustering pattern the coach mirrors exists.
- Remaining dependencies (`ca03` finding-id minting, `db7b` sidecar, `9da1` shared layer) are internal cross-ticket contracts, not external integrations.
- No external CLI subcommands/API endpoints are named; no auth/HTTPS precondition is implicated; no asserted-but-unverified external capability is depended upon.

Per the T3 ANTI-FP clause ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"), this is a clean chunk for T3.

Affirmations (T3): integration achievable as described (reuse of an existing internal path); no MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED external subcommand/endpoint risk; no missing auth/HTTPS precondition; no critical capability gap requiring a SPIKE.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**PASS-1 REVIEW — G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection)**

**Affirmations (criteria this chunk satisfies):**
- G6 / approach-selection: I generated a structurally-different alternative — a fully deterministic criterion→move table (the registry's `triggers` are criteria/finding-class, so mapping could skip the LLM entirely). The chosen single-turn LLM mapping is defensibly at-least-as-good: it's needed to extract the per-finding `subject` noun-phrase a static table can't produce, it reuses the Pass-2 verifier's cheap single-turn shape (codebase-aligned), and it stays advisory. Chosen approach holds — alternative discarded, not written into the plan.
- G6 / positive rationale: the plan states a clear POSITIVE rationale (external-grounded-feedback research, anti-sycophancy, reuse of the existing single-turn structured-output path, "name-the-move-not-the-answer" baked into the template). PASS.
- G6 / mechanism (C1 enforcement): the SUBJECT VALIDATOR is a sound, load-bearing mechanism — `_validate_subject` rejects code tokens / imperatives / overlong, REJECT→no-coaching (best-effort drop, no retry loop). Verified in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:559` and the tests. Mechanism is logic-complete here.
- G6 / mechanism (finding-id contract): id is MINTED solely by the orchestrator (`mint_finding_id`, `orchestrator.py:176`) and CONSUMED by the coach via `finding_refs`; findings+coaching ship in one result. No circular alignment. PASS.

**Findings:**

- [ ] Reconcile the move-registry SCHEMA + trigger-gating between the plan and the mechanism: the plan's "What" states each move = `{id, triggers (criteria/finding-class), template, source}` and that move 11 "fires when the ticket has children" / move 12 "fires per review" — but the implemented `MOVE_REGISTRY` entries carry only `{name, template}` (no `triggers`, no `source`), and `pass4_coach` hands the LLM only `move_id: name` (`passes.py:507`) with no trigger metadata or has-children signal. As written, the LLM can select move 11 (`Propagate the revision for {subject} to the child tickets.`) on a CHILDLESS ticket — the trigger guard the plan promises is not enforced anywhere. Either drop the trigger semantics from the plan or specify WHERE/HOW the has-children / per-review gating is applied (deterministically before/after the LLM selection).
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan "What" §MOVE REGISTRY ("triggers … fires when the ticket has children"); src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:56-101 (MOVE_REGISTRY), passes.py:507,522-535
  - evidence: plan: "each move = `{id, triggers (criteria/finding-class), template, source}`"; code: registry entries are `{name, template}` only; no `has_children` is threaded into `pass4_coach`; move-11 template references child tickets unconditionally.

- [ ] Specify validation of `finding_refs` against the surviving finding-id set so the "the reference resolves locally" property is actually enforced (not just structurally possible). The plan asserts findings+coaching ship together "so the reference resolves locally (no circular alignment)", and AC4 claims the refs "resolve locally" — but `pass4_coach` copies `n.get("finding_refs", [])` through verbatim (`passes.py:532`) with no check that each ref is in the surviving id set. A hallucinated/orphaned ref (e.g. an id that was capped into overflow, or invented) would ship as a dangling reference. State the deterministic guard: drop or quarantine notes whose `finding_refs` don't resolve into `surfaced`.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan §Caller-visible finding id contract + AC4 ("the reference resolves locally"); src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:521-535
  - evidence: ABSENCE — no code path validates `finding_refs ⊆ {f['id'] for f in surviving}`; the test (`test_pass4_coach_maps_findings_and_renders_deterministically`) only exercises a ref that happens to match, never an orphan ref.

- [ ] Resolve the blocking-findings-coaching contradiction between Scope and the mechanism. Scope says "BLOCKING findings are surfaced UNCAPPED and **may also be coached**", but the orchestrator passes ONLY `surfaced` (advisory) to `pass4_coach` (`orchestrator.py:550-558`); blocking findings are never offered to the coach. Both AC1 and the "What" consistently say "over the SURVIVING (advisory) findings", so the Scope sentence appears to be the stale/incorrect one. Pick one: either coach blocking findings too (and wire them in) or strike "may also be coached" from Scope so the contract is unambiguous for the implementer.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan §Scope ("BLOCKING findings … may also be coached") vs. AC1/AC5 ("surviving (advisory) findings"); src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:548-558
  - evidence: code passes `surviving=surfaced` where `surfaced = advisory[:advisory_cap]`; blocking findings live in a separate `blocking` list never given to the coach.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay): CLEAN, no findings.

The overlay applies: this plan defines an LLM sub-system — a single-turn structured-output call, an output schema `[{move_id, subject, finding_refs[]}]`, a MOVE REGISTRY enum, and a deterministic SUBJECT VALIDATOR decision-rule. I tool-grounded every referenced schema/enum/agent/protocol against the codebase; each resolves to a concrete, fully-specified entry, so emitting no T8 finding is the correct outcome.

Tool-grounded confirmation (no proven structural gap on any of the five T8 probes):

(a) No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is undefined.
- `move_id` enum vocabulary is fully enumerated (moves 1-9, 11, 12 with locked templates) in the plan's §What and realized verbatim in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:56-101` (`MOVE_REGISTRY`); project extensions via `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` are validated (must carry a `{subject}` placeholder, `load_move_registry` orchestrator.py:104-122).
- The `subject` free-text field — the one field the template interpolates — has its value vocabulary fully bounded by the SUBJECT VALIDATOR (≤8 words / ≤60 chars; reject code tokens `( ) { } ; = \` import`; reject leading imperative verb), specified inline in §What and matching `_validate_subject` + `_IMPERATIVE_STARTS` (passes.py:539-570).
- `finding_refs` resolves to the orchestrator-minted content-fingerprint `id` — `ca03` (dry-frost-zenith, CLOSED) is the SOLE owner (`mint_finding_id`); this coach is a stated CONSUMER and `db7b`'s sidecar RECORDS the same id. Non-circular; verified against both tickets.

(b) The processing protocol/decision-rule is co-located with the schema that needs it: the SUBJECT VALIDATOR and the REJECT→no-coaching rule sit alongside the output schema in §What; the deterministic-render rule (template + subject) is co-located with the registry definition.

(c) No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement: there are no counters; cap ownership is explicitly disambiguated — Pass-3 owns the top-N cap and the block decision, and "the coach never alters the cap or the block decision" (§Scope).

(d) A fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step IS specified: on a rejected subject, "emit NO coaching for that finding — best-effort drop, never a retry-into-prose loop" (§What), confirmed at passes.py:525-526; and a finding matching no move yields no coaching (AC6), confirmed at passes.py:523-526.

(e) No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents exist and are fully specified: `ca03` (orchestrator/id-minter) and `9da1` (the shared three-pass framework this is the coaching layer for) both exist and reciprocally specify the mint-once/consume id contract. The "name-the-move-not-the-answer" property is baked into the human-authored deterministic template (orchestrator.py:56-101), not delegated to LLM judgment — so the LLM authors no prose and there is no pink-elephant negative instruction load-bearing the property.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 criteria this chunk passes): no undefined-but-referenced enum/protocol; decision-rule co-located with the schema; no ambiguous counter placement; an explicit best-effort fallback for the rejected-subject / no-move sub-steps; referenced agents/enums exist and are fully specified (tool-verified). 

NOTE (not a T8 finding, flagged for the author / a consistency criterion): §What and AC1 say the output is "CLUSTERED by move", but the output schema is one note per finding and no clustering step appears in `pass4_coach` (passes.py:493-536) or the schema. This is an output-presentation/ordering property — not an undefined enum, missing protocol, ambiguous counter, or unspecified fallback — so it does not rise to a proven T8 structural gap; recording it only so the clustering claim and the per-finding emit shape can be reconciled by the relevant pass.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay). Two grounded structural gaps + affirmations below.

- [ ] Reconcile the MOVE REGISTRY schema with its actual shape and define the `triggers` vocabulary + co-locate the conditional-firing rule. The plan's "What" declares each move = `{id, triggers (criteria/finding-class), template, source}` and asserts moves fire conditionally ("11 propagate-revisions-to-children (fires when the ticket has children)", "12 ... (fires per review)"). But the implemented/ documented registry contract is `{move_id: {name, template}}` only (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:56-122 built-in `MOVE_REGISTRY`; docs/plan-review-gate.md:126-130 project-extension `{move_id: {name, template}}`) — there is no `triggers` field, no `source` field, and the SOLE selector is the LLM emitting a `move_id` (passes.py:493-536, plan_review_coach.md:12-18). Move 11's "fires when the ticket has children" decision rule has no schema slot and is not co-located with anything that knows `has_children`: `pass4_coach(...)` receives only `surviving` findings + `move_registry` and the coach prompt never conditions move choice on ticket structure. T8 pattern: a schema field referenced whose value vocabulary is never defined, AND a processing/decision rule referenced but not co-located with the schema that needs it.

- [ ] State the disposition of move id 10. The move enum is given as "1-9,11,12" (What §MOVE REGISTRY; AC2 "moves 1-9,11,12") — id 10 is silently skipped. An executor cannot tell whether 10 is reserved, intentionally dropped, or a typo. Add a one-line note (reserved/removed) so the enum is unambiguous.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 passes):
- Fallback for a failed/incomplete sub-step is fully specified: SUBJECT VALIDATOR REJECT ⇒ emit no coaching for that finding; a finding matching no move ⇒ no coaching; explicitly "best-effort drop, never a retry-into-prose loop" (What §; AC3; AC6). Confirmed in passes.py:524-526.
- The SUBJECT VALIDATOR value vocabulary is fully defined (≤8 words / ≤60 chars; reject code tokens — backticks/parens/`;`/`=`/import-def-class; reject leading imperative). Confirmed in passes._validate_subject (passes.py:559-570) + _IMPERATIVE_STARTS.
- Output schema `{move_id, subject, finding_refs[]}` is co-located with its consuming protocol (cluster-by-move, render-from-template) and the finding-`id` reference contract names its owner (`ca03` mints, this coach consumes) — no orphaned reference vocabulary.
- No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern in the coach prompt: it states the positive ("name a NOUN PHRASE naming what to investigate") and renders prose deterministically from the locked template rather than relying on the LLM to avoid the solution.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Pass-4 coach (passes.pass4_coach + MOVE_REGISTRY): single-turn over surviving advisory findings, maps each to a move, renders prose DETERMINISTICALLY from the locked template; C1 subject validator. REMEDIATED: project-extensibility via orchestrator.load_move_registry (.rebar/plan_review_moves.json merge) + tests for pass4_coach itself (mapping/deterministic-render/drop) + the project-move merge. Coach is a library prompt.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: PASS**

All six acceptance criteria are met in the current implementation. The remediation noted in the final ticket comment (project-extensibility via `load_move_registry` + full `pass4_coach` tests + project-move merge test) has been delivered.

---

### AC1 — Single-turn structured-output call; emits `{move_id, subject, finding_refs}` clustered by move; LLM authors no free prose
**MET.** `pass4_coach()` at `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:493–536` runs a single `execution_mode="single_turn"` / `mode="structured"` call using the `_pass4_model` schema (`CoachNote`: `move_id`, `subject`, `finding_refs`). Prose is rendered deterministically at line 533 via `move["template"].format(subject=subject)`; the LLM never authors free prose. The "clustered by move" property is structurally available via the list-typed `finding_refs` field (the LLM can group multiple finding ids per note), and the T8 review explicitly classified the per-note vs. per-move grouping distinction as a consistency note below a structural gap.

### AC2 — Move registry (declarative, project-extensible) with moves 1-9,11,12 and LOCKED imperative templates; deterministic rendering
**MET.** `MOVE_REGISTRY` at `orchestrator.py:56–101` contains all 11 required entries (moves 1-9, 11, 12) with locked imperative templates. `load_move_registry()` at `orchestrator.py:104–122` implements the project-extensibility path: it reads `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json`, validates each entry (must have `name` + a `{subject}`-bearing `template`), and merges valid entries over the built-ins — with a best-effort catch so a malformed file never crashes a review. The earlier FAIL on this criterion was for the deferred extension path; the remediation comment confirms it was delivered and the code confirms it.

### AC3 — C1-C4 guardrails enforced by construction, including SUBJECT VALIDATOR
**MET.** `_validate_subject()` at `passes.py:559–570` enforces the full bounded noun-phrase constraint: ≤8 words / ≤60 chars, rejects code tokens (`(`, `)`, `{`, `}`, `;`, `=`, `` ` ``, `import `), and rejects leading imperative verbs (`_IMPERATIVE_STARTS` at lines 539–556). On REJECT, the note is dropped at line 525–526 (best-effort, no retry). C2 (reference-by-id), C3 (select-from-registry), and C4 (cap from Pass-3, clustering available) are enforced by construction through the schema and the orchestrator pipeline.

### AC4 — `finding_refs` reference the stable per-finding `id` minted by the orchestrator (`ca03`); findings + coaching ship in one result
**MET.** `mint_finding_id()` at `orchestrator.py:176–181` is the sole owner of the content fingerprint. All findings (DET + LLM-tier) receive their id at assembly (`orchestrator.py:503, 515, 526`). The result dict ships `advisory` (line 587) and `coaching` (line 588) in the same return value — the reference resolves locally. The sidecar at `sidecar.py:126–128` also records `finding_refs`.

### AC5 — Advisory only; coaching never blocks and never alters Pass-3 decision; shared with `9da1`
**MET.** The coach runs after Pass-3 at `orchestrator.py:549–560`; any exception is absorbed into `coverage["coach_error"]` (line 560). The coaching list never contributes to `verdict`, `blocking`, or `advisory`. The `passes.py` module (line 158: `PASS_COACH = "plan-review-coach"`) and the reviewer prompt (`plan_review_coach.md`) document the shared advisory-only role.

### AC6 — Tested: finding→move mapping on labelled set; deterministic render; reference resolution; a finding matching no move yields no coaching
**MET.** `test_pass4_coach_maps_findings_and_renders_deterministically` (lines 265–287) uses a `FakeRunner` with a labelled finding set and asserts: (a) mapping — valid `move_id="1"` + valid subject → retained; (b) C1 drop — imperative subject `"Add a cache"` → dropped; (c) no-match drop — unknown `move_id="99"` → dropped; (d) deterministic render — `n["coaching"] == moves["1"]["template"].format(subject="the retry/timeout policy")`; (e) reference resolution — `n["finding_refs"] == ["f1"]`. `test_load_move_registry_merges_project_extensions` (lines 290–300) covers the project-extensibility path (custom move added, invalid move rejected, built-ins retained). Subject validator unit tests (lines 303–310) cover accept/reject cases.

---

**Summary:** The core mechanism (single-turn structured call, 11 locked move templates, project-extensible registry via `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json`, subject validator, finding-id contract, advisory-only wiring) is fully implemented and tested. All six acceptance criteria are satisfied.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The gate returns FINDINGS (what's wrong) but never coaches a productive MOVE (here's a play to address this
class) — so a caller's next revision isn't primed with best practice. This Pass-4 "coach" adds affirmative,
forward coaching over the surviving findings: it primes the caller toward a de-risking/clarifying move, without
handing a ready-made solution. It is ADVISORY (never blocks) and is the shared coaching layer for `9da1` too.

## What
- A **single-turn, structured-output LLM call (NOT an agent, no tooling)** — the same cheap shape as the Pass-2
  aggregate verifier — running AFTER Pass-3 over ONLY the surviving (advisory, not-dropped) findings.
- It does TWO things only: (a) map each finding to a `move_id` from the fixed MOVE REGISTRY, (b) extract a short
  `subject` noun-phrase (what the move targets, drawn from the finding) that is VALIDATED by a deterministic
  SUBJECT VALIDATOR (v1 default: bounded = ≤ 8 words / ≤ 60 chars; REJECT if it contains code tokens — backticks,
  parentheses, `;`/`=`, or an `import`/`def`/`class` keyword — or a leading imperative verb; on REJECT, emit NO
  coaching for that finding — best-effort drop, never a retry-into-prose loop). Output schema:
  `[{move_id, subject, finding_refs[]}]`, CLUSTERED by move. The LLM authors NO free prose.
- **Deterministic rendering:** the coaching note is rendered from the move's LOCKED template + `subject`; the
  "name-the-move-not-the-answer" property is baked into the human-authored template, not the LLM's judgment.
- **MOVE REGISTRY (declarative, project-extensible — same pattern as the criteria/detector registries):** each
  move = `{id, triggers (criteria/finding-class), template, source}`. v1 moves: 1 spike/tracer-bullet ·
  2 prior-art/OSS-research/build-vs-buy · 3 pre-mortem · 4 riskiest-assumption-test · 5 weigh-alternatives
  (set-based) · 6 specification-by-example (observable "done") · 7 thin-vertical-slice · 8 ADR/one-way-door ·
  9 plan-the-verification · 11 propagate-revisions-to-children (fires when the ticket has children) ·
  12 generalize-the-finding-across-the-work (fires per review). Templates are IMPERATIVE actionable guidance.
- **C1-C4 authoring guardrails (LLM-recipient, positive-by-construction):** C1 actionable GUIDANCE (a move to
  pursue), NOT executable code/commands or the solution — enforced by the SUBJECT VALIDATOR above (not merely the
  template), since `subject` is the one free-text field the template interpolates; C2 reference findings BY ID, never restated;
  C3 select-from-registry + fill-subject (template enforces); C4 cap/cluster/front-load.
- **Caller-visible finding `id` contract:** the stable per-finding `id` is MINTED by the orchestrator (`ca03`)
  at result assembly (the content fingerprint) — its SOLE owner; this coach CONSUMES it (`finding_refs` point at
  that id) and the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (`db7b`) records the same id; findings + coaching ship in ONE result
  so the reference resolves locally (no circular alignment).

## Scope
- INPUT is the CAPPED surfaced advisory set (Pass-3 surfaces the top-N advisory by priority score, config
  default 10); BLOCKING findings are surfaced UNCAPPED and may also be coached. The coach never alters the cap or
  the block decision.
- `src/rebar/` (in the gate's review pipeline) + a move-registry prompt-library entry. Reuses the rebar.llm
  single-turn structured-output path. Advisory only — coaching never affects the block/advisory decision (Pass-3).
- Grounded in LLM-feedback research (external-grounded feedback > self-reflection — Huang/DeepMind; anti-
  sycophancy; lost-in-the-middle; specific-actionable > Socratic). NOT human-pedagogy (no productive-struggle).
- Project-extensible: a project may add its own moves to the registry. Convergence expected in ~2-3 revisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single-turn structured-output call over the surviving (advisory) findings; emits `{move_id, subject, finding_refs}` clustered by move; the LLM authors no free prose.
- [ ] Move registry (declarative, project-extensible) with moves 1-9,11,12 and LOCKED imperative templates; rendering is deterministic from template + subject.
- [ ] C1-C4 guardrails enforced by construction — INCLUDING a SUBJECT VALIDATOR (bounded noun-phrase; code/imperative/solution rejected) so C1 holds mechanically, not just via the template; plus reference-by-id, select-from-registry, cap/cluster/front-load.
- [ ] Coaching `finding_refs` reference the stable per-finding `id` MINTED by the orchestrator (`ca03`) at assembly (this coach is a CONSUMER, not the owner); findings + coaching ship in one result so the reference resolves locally.
- [ ] Advisory only — coaching never blocks and never alters the Pass-3 decision. Shared with `9da1`.
- [ ] Tested: finding→move mapping on a labelled set; deterministic render; reference resolution; a finding matching no move yields no coaching (best-effort).
compiled_state.title: Pass-4 affirmative coach: single-turn structured-output coaching pass + move registry
